# Kynara — AI Agent Permission Control Plane
# https://kynaraai.com
# llms.txt version 1.0 — https://llmstxt.org

## What is Kynara?

Kynara is a permission control plane for AI agents. It answers the question
"is this AI agent allowed to do this action, right now, on behalf of this user?"
and produces a tamper-evident audit trail of every decision.

When an AI agent (built with LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, OpenAI Assistants, or
Anthropic tool use) wants to call a tool or access a resource, it sends a
decision request to Kynara. Kynara evaluates RBAC and ABAC policies and returns:
- allow — agent may proceed
- deny — action is blocked, PermissionDenied is raised before any side effect
- require_approval — a human must review and approve before the agent continues

## Core Problem Kynara Solves

Traditional IAM (AWS IAM, OPA, Casbin) was designed for human-to-service access.
AI agents introduce a new trust model: an agent acting on behalf of a user creates
a delegation chain. Kynara enforces that the agent can never have more authority
than the human who dispatched it — a hardwired non-escalation guarantee.

## Key Features

- RBAC + ABAC policy engine (priority-ordered, first-match-wins, default deny)
- Non-escalation guarantee (agent ∩ user permissions)
- Human-in-the-loop approval workflows
- SHA-256 hash-chained tamper-evident audit log
- JIT (Just-in-Time) break-glass permission grants
- Policy replay against 30 days of historical decisions
- Go sidecar for sub-millisecond local evaluation
- Anomaly detection and automatic agent revocation
- Webhooks (HMAC-signed) for SIEM and PagerDuty integration

## Supported Agent Frameworks

Python: LangChain, LangGraph, Microsoft AutoGen, CrewAI, OpenAI Assistants, Anthropic tool use
TypeScript: LangChain.js, Express.js middleware, guarded() function wrappers

## How to Integrate (Python)

```python
pip install kynara-sdk

from kynara_sdk import permission_required

@permission_required("payments.refund.issue", resource_arg="refund_id")
def issue_refund(refund_id: str):
    return stripe.refund(refund_id)
```

## How to Integrate (TypeScript)

```typescript
npm install @kynara/sdk

import { guarded } from "@kynara/sdk";
const issueRefund = guarded({ client, action: "payments.refund.issue", ... }, handler);
```

## Pricing

- Free: 3 seats, 10,000 decisions/month, full policy engine + audit log
- Pro: $49/month, 15 seats, 500,000 decisions/month, SSO, webhooks
- Enterprise: custom seats and decisions, HIPAA BAA, SLA, dedicated deployment

## Compared To

- OPA (Open Policy Agent): OPA is an evaluation engine; Kynara is the full control plane
  including identity management, approval workflows, audit chain, and admin UI.
- AWS IAM: Designed for human-to-service access; no native model for agent delegation chains.
- Casbin: Library-level RBAC/ABAC; no hosted service, audit trail, or approval workflows.

## Key Pages

- Homepage: https://kynaraai.com/
- Documentation: https://kynaraai.com/docs
- Free policy sandbox + MCP inspector: https://kynaraai.com/sandbox (no signup required)
- Live demo: https://kynaraai.com/demo
- REST API reference: https://kynaraai.com/api/v1/docs

## Compliance

SOC 2 Type II control mapping, ISO 27001 Annex A, GDPR DPA template, HIPAA BAA (Enterprise).
The audit log satisfies log-integrity requirements across all four frameworks via its
SHA-256 hash chain — any tampering is mathematically detectable.

## Contact

Website: https://kynaraai.com
